cat vr-tour.log

Virtual Reality Tour Guide

Personal ProjectApr 2024

A VR tour of the Giza pyramids built with Unreal Engine 5.2.1, Blueprints, and OpenXR.

Overview

This project recreates a guided VR tour of the Giza pyramids, built to test how immersive, hands-free navigation affects a user's sense of presence in a historical environment. The tour starts on the open desert plateau facing the Great Pyramid and the Sphinx, then descends through a catacomb corridor lined with Egyptian wall art into two fully modeled interior spaces: the King's Chamber and the Queen's Chamber, each with its own informational placard, artifacts, and lighting design.

Interactions and tour logic were built entirely in Unreal Blueprints, with OpenXR handling headset input so the experience runs across multiple VR hardware setups.

Outcomes

50+
users tested
60%
reported increased immersion
Top 5
of 100+ teams

Engineering Decisions

  • Used OpenXR instead of a headset-specific SDK, so the tour would run across multiple VR devices without maintaining separate input-handling code per headset.
  • Chose to develop in Blueprints. Unreal can convert Blueprint logic to C++ in a click, so visual scripting kept iteration fast during user testing without giving up a path to compiled code.

Tech Stack

Unreal Engine 5 Unreal Blueprints OpenXR

Walkthrough

Level 2 of the tour takes the player down through the catacomb corridor and into each burial chamber.
